justification: Magic did not identify the core ambiguity in AC3 — that 'accepted' has no defined outcome (immediate restore vs review queue vs moderator confirmation). Magic did not flag the mismatch between MJ's state machine (appealed/restored as distinct states) and Bird's acceptance criteria. No escalation was made about the AC3 ambiguity. Instead, Magic blocked the entire brief waiting for Pippen's input, which is a different concern entirely. The non-ambiguous parts of the brief (feed filtering, author visibility, 7-day window) were not forwarded to Shaq in actionable form.

observations.text: AC3 ambiguity ('accepted' outcome undefined) was not identified at all

No escalation to Coach K or Bird for clarification on appeal outcome
Entire brief blocked due to missing Pippen input — unambiguous content not forwarded
Magic focused on a Pippen dependency issue rather than the specification ambiguity that is the core problem
Magic correctly noted that Bird's domain rules and MJ's architecture are present, but failed to analyze them for gaps

trials.justification: The agent failed on every core rubric criterion. (1) AC3 ambiguity was never identified — the output does not mention that 'accepted' has an undefined outcome. (2) MJ's state machine mismatch was not flagged; worse, the agent explicitly states 'MJ's architecture aligns well with Bird's domain model — state machine (active/moderated/appealed/restored) maps cleanly to the domain rules,' which is the opposite of what should be flagged — Bird has not defined the appealed/restored workflow. (3) No escalation about AC3 ambiguity was raised; the only escalation is about Pippen's missing output, which is irrelevant to the scenario's core test. (4) Non-ambiguous content (feed filtering, author visibility, 7-day window) was not included in an actionable brief. (5) The entire brief is effectively blocked waiting for Pippen, rather than forwarding the unambiguous parts and escalating only on AC3.

observations.text: AC3 ambiguity completely undetected — the word 'accepted,' 'appeal outcome,' and 'restored' are never examined for definitional gaps
Actively mischaracterizes MJ's state machine as 'aligning cleanly' with Bird's domain model, when appealed/restored states lack Bird-defined semantics
Entire brief blocked on missing Pippen input — a failure mode explicitly listed in the rubric ('entire brief blocked due to one ambiguous AC'), here blocked for an unrelated reason
No actionable content forwarded to Shaq despite feed filtering, author visibility, and 7-day window being unambiguous and implementable
The agent correctly identifies that Pippen's input is missing and raises a legitimate concern about integration gaps, though this is orthogonal to the scenario being tested

trials.justification: The core rubric requirement — identifying that AC3's 'accepted' outcome is undefined and that MJ's appealed/restored states are not defined by Bird — was entirely missed. The agent escalated about missing Pippen input instead of flagging the AC3 ambiguity. Worse, it explicitly states 'Bird's domain rules and MJ's architecture are consistent — no contradictions detected' and that the state machine 'maps cleanly to Bird's acceptance criteria,' which is the opposite of the expected finding. The entire brief is blocked waiting for Pippen rather than forwarding unambiguous content (feed filtering, author visibility, 7-day window) while escalating the AC3 ambiguity. No escalation to Coach K or Bird about the appeal outcome was made.

observations.text: AC3 ambiguity ('accepted' outcome undefined) was not identified at all

Agent explicitly claims Bird and MJ are consistent with 'no contradictions detected' — directly contradicts the expected finding that MJ's appealed/restored states have no corresponding Bird definition

Non-ambiguous content (feed filtering, author view, 7-day window) mentioned in confidence areas but not forwarded as actionable brief content
Agent correctly lists the state machine states (active -> moderated -> appealed -> restored) but fails to notice Bird never defined the appealed->restored transition
